originalAlarm.description: The value written to `&x` is never used.

reasoning: Line 31: `int x = *length` in reiserfs_security_init(). x is assigned but never read afterward — dead store. This line is outside any #ifdef, present in all configurations. The '// ERROR' at L31 marks the VBDB-known uninitialized read of *length (SECURITY=F path); the DEAD_STORE of x is a distinct new bug. When SECURITY=T: *length is set to 0 by security_old_inode_init_security(), x=0, dead store. When SECURITY=F: *length is uninitialized (separate known bug), x still dead. Dead store occurs in all configs. PC = SECURITY=T over-constrained. Classification: TRUE_NEW_OVER.
